item.title: A True Crime Case of Family Tragedy
item.description: True Crime Recaps explores a case involving a family tragedy and a second chance at life.

media:content.media:title: A True Crime Case of Family Tragedy
media:content.media:description: True Crime Recaps explores a case involving a family tragedy and a second chance at life.

item.title: Arrest Made in Ashley Okland Cold Case
item.description: Ashley Okland, 27, was found dead inside a West Des Moines townhome she was hosting an open house at on April 8, 2011. The case went unsolved for nearly 15 years. In March 2026, Kristin Ramsey, 53, was indicted by a grand jury and charged with first-degree murder. No motive has been publicly disclosed. The case is pending trial.

media:content.media:title: Arrest Made in Ashley Okland Cold Case
media:content.media:description: Ashley Okland, 27, was found dead inside a West Des Moines townhome she was hosting an open house at on April 8, 2011. The case went unsolved for nearly 15 years. In March 2026, Kristin Ramsey, 53, was indicted by a grand jury and charged with first-degree murder. No motive has been publicly disclosed. The case is pending trial.

item.title: What Happened to Leah Roberts on Desolation Peak?
item.description: Leah Roberts, 23, left her home in Durham, North Carolina on March 9, 2000, on a solo road trip to the Pacific Northwest inspired by author Jack Kerouac. She left a note and rent money for her roommate and told no one where she was going. Nine days later, her wrecked Jeep Cherokee was found at the bottom of an embankment off a remote logging road in Whatcom County, Washington. No blood was found inside the vehicle, and investigators later determined a wire in the engine had been cut, raising questions about whether the car had been occupied during the crash. Unidentified male DNA was recovered from items in the vehicle, but no suspect has ever been identified. Leah Roberts has not been found, and her case remains open and unsolved.

media:content.media:title: What Happened to Leah Roberts on Desolation Peak?
media:content.media:description: Leah Roberts, 23, left her home in Durham, North Carolina on March 9, 2000, on a solo road trip to the Pacific Northwest inspired by author Jack Kerouac. She left a note and rent money for her roommate and told no one where she was going. Nine days later, her wrecked Jeep Cherokee was found at the bottom of an embankment off a remote logging road in Whatcom County, Washington. No blood was found inside the vehicle, and investigators later determined a wire in the engine had been cut, raising questions about whether the car had been occupied during the crash. Unidentified male DNA was recovered from items in the vehicle, but no suspect has ever been identified. Leah Roberts has not been found, and her case remains open and unsolved.
